ip_outcome_note: Verified: depicted figure is Liliana Vess. Scryfall flavor_text explicitly names Liliana ("Liliana whispered to whatever consciousness Oketra and Bontu had left."); Scryfall flags story_spotlight: true (canonical War of the Spark narrative moment); multiple lore sources (MTG Wiki, Wargamer, Upcomer story recap) corroborate that this is the moment Liliana, freed from Bolas' contract, turns the God-Eternals Oketra and Bontu against him. Liliana is Wizards-internal IP (no external-crossover risk), so no ## IP callout required, only ip_verified flip and Vision-warning reconciliation.

trivia_bullets.text: Despark is a designated DailyMTG Story Spotlight card from War of the Spark; the art depicts the moment Liliana Vess, freed from Nicol Bolas' contract, commands the corrupted God-Eternals Oketra and Bontu (the two cracked god-statue heads behind her) to turn on Bolas himself. The exiled-permanent mechanic mirrors the narrative beat of unmaking the Elder Dragon's apparatus of control. [Scryfall: story_spotlight=true], [Wargamer: War of the Spark story guide], [MTG Wiki: War of the Spark/Event]
sources: MTG Wiki: War of the Spark/Event
Upcomer: War of the Spark Story Recap Act III

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "Liliana whispered to whatever consciousness Oketra and Bontu had left. 'You are the gods. He is the usurper. You know what to do.'" — direct quote of Liliana's command to the God-Eternals at the turning point of the Bolas confrontation. [Scryfall]

Art by Slawomir Maniak, who illustrated several War of the Spark planeswalker-adjacent moments. The card was previewed by The Vorthos Cast in April 2019, a podcast focused on Magic's story/art, consistent with the card's status as a story-spotlight piece. [Scryfall: artist + preview source]

Despark is a heavily-played Orzhov removal staple in Commander: EDHREC lists it in roughly 30% of Orzhov (W/B) decks (~99K of 327K Orzhov decks tracked). Two-mana instant-speed exile that ignores indestructible and most protection clauses is the appeal; the mana-value-4-or-greater restriction is the cost. [EDHREC: Despark card page], [EDHREC: Orzhov staples]

Despark belongs to the War of the Spark removal cycle alongside other Orzhov narrative-removal pieces; the set's design conceit was that every rare/mythic includes a planeswalker, making story-spotlight uncommons like this one the primary vehicle for non-planeswalker plot beats. [MTG Wiki: War of the Spark]
sources: MTG Wiki: War of the Spark

related_cards.name: God-Eternal Oketra
related_cards.note: One of the two God-Eternals Liliana commands in the Despart moment; the pale-stone bearded statue head in the Despark art is consistent with Oketra's iconography.

related_cards.name: God-Eternal Bontu
related_cards.note: The other God-Eternal in the Despart moment; per the WAR story recap, Bontu is the one that bit Bolas and harvested his spark before exploding.

related_cards.name: Liliana, Dreadhorde General
related_cards.note: Liliana's mythic-rare planeswalker card from the same set, depicting her commanding the Dreadhorde — the broader Bolas-war narrative arc.

related_cards.name: Anguished Unmaking
related_cards.note: The most-compared Orzhov exile-anything-permanent instant; Despark is the cheaper, mana-value-gated cousin in the same removal lineage.

related_cards.name: Utter End
related_cards.note: Predecessor Orzhov exile-any-permanent instant at 4 mana; Despark trades the no-restriction clause for a steep 2-mana cost when targets qualify.
